page: Amazon Web Services r5.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is consistently positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression tests. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound operations fall within the middle 50% average tier. This includes stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance, alongside Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. Large-scale LLM inference on a 70B model is poor, falling into the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r5.8xlarge offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-thread CPU performance. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core efficiency, meaning workloads bound by single-threaded execution will experience slower processing speeds. However, the dedicated vCPU allocation and Nitro hypervisor ensure consistent resource availability for multi-threaded tasks.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates that while single-core performance is weak, multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations consistently rank in the average tier. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-dense workloads that do not demand high single-threaded CPU speeds. The instance is suited for database operations, in-memory caching, multi-threaded processing, and software compilation.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high RAM density and dedicated vCPUs for database and multi-threaded caching workloads.

256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 8.0 GB per physical core
